title: Portable 2x4 and Pegboard Tool Rack
summary: A caster-mounted A-frame tool rack faced in pegboard — rolls between rooms, garage bays, or job sites.
description:
Pegboard is the most flexible way to store hand tools, but it's usually screwed to a wall — fine until the tools need to move with you, between a garage, a job site, or a house you're mid-move out of.

This rack puts pegboard on a rolling 2x4 frame instead. The **5° leg angle on both A-frame sides** is what keeps the whole thing stable standing on its own, top-heavy with tools, without needing a wide, space-hogging footprint.

Add scrap plywood shelves between the frames for anything that doesn't hang on a hook, and don't cut corners on the casters — this thing gets heavier than it looks once it's loaded.

tools.note: Or a miter saw — handles both the square and 5° angled cuts.

materials.name: Swivel casters, 2" (2 fixed, 2 locking)
materials.note: The two locking casters matter — a loaded rack that can't be locked in place will roll while you're working off it.

cutList.note: 5° angle on both ends, parallel to each other.
5° angle on both ends, perpendicular to the legs.

steps.title: Cut the frame pieces
steps.body: Cut the four legs and two top braces at 5°, and the four base pieces square, per the cut list. Set your saw's angle once and cut every angled piece before changing it back — that's what keeps both A-frame sides matching.

steps.title: Build the two A-frame sides
steps.body: Drill pocket holes in one end of each 6ft leg and assemble two A-frame sides, joining the legs to the base pieces at the bottom and the top braces near the top.

steps.title: Attach the pegboard
steps.body: Cut the pegboard to fit and screw it to the front face of each A-frame side, then install your pegboard hooks in whatever layout suits your tools.

steps.title: Add shelves if you want them
steps.body: Measure and cut scrap plywood to fit between the two A-frame sides for anything that doesn't hang on a hook — a battery charger, a parts bin, a roll of tape.

steps.title: Mount the casters
steps.body:
Attach two fixed and two locking swivel casters to the base, one at each corner.

Check that both locking casters actually engage before you load the rack — a rack that rolls away mid-repair is worse than no rack at all.
